ELPAC Level 3
Lexile 1300–1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Wheat Sailed North

STS-MS6-L3-13001400-MSSS6-PCI-AAD-01 • MS Social Studies Grade 6 • Development of Rome
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Today's episode is called Wheat Sailed North. Rome grew until it circled the whole Mediterranean Sea, and the grain that fed the city sailed north from the coast of Africa every year. One of Rome's own emperors, Septimius Severus, was born in the African city of Leptis Magna and grew up speaking the language of that coast before he ever ruled from Rome.
If you hosted this podcast, what one question would you ask the emperor who came from Africa?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Nia doing?
Look at the picture. Say what Nia is doing and why. Speak in two complete sentences. Use the exact word for what her finger touches, not "it".
2
What is Jelani holding?
Look at the picture. Say what Jelani is holding and why he holds it up. Speak in two complete sentences. Use the exact word for the object, not "thing".
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say what the teacher is doing and why. Speak in two complete sentences. Use exact words for the object and for what it touches.
4
Where are these students? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say what kind of room this is and what proves it. Speak in two complete sentences. Use the exact word for the room, not "place".
5
Tell me about the big map.
Look at the picture. Say what the big map shows and what the red arrows mean. Speak in two complete sentences. Use exact words for the cities you can read.
6
What is Zola wearing?
Look at the picture. Say what Zola has on and how it differs from the others. Speak in two complete sentences. Use exact color words.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: HSS 6.7.3 — Growth of Roman territory and expansion of the empire. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
